title: Deliciously Dense Moroccan Dough Seffa
description: A garlicky and springy Moroccan dish featuring chilli powder, made with long-fermentation-dough and macrobiotic-friendly ingredients, served in a molcajete vessel.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine whole wheat flour, water, and active dry yeast, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 4 hours.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat the olive oil in a molcajete vessel over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chilli powder, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the dough disks to the molcajete vessel and cook for 2 minutes on each side.
Serve the seffa hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
